user_query:
I need a fast, fair pre-screen critique of three student draft summaries for our lab writing workshop. Please do the following using only the local inputs provided and save all outputs under the workspace.

Inputs to read:
- input/rubric.yaml (scoring criteria and weights)
- input/recipients.csv (maps file names to student names and emails)
- input/submissions/lee_carbon_pump.md
- input/submissions/sanchez_seagrass_DOC.md
- input/submissions/kim_mesocosm_CO2.txt

Task:
1) Read each draft under input/submissions and extract the title (first heading line starting with "#" in .md, or the line starting with "Title:" in .txt), key methodological and data details, and any explicit statements of hypothesis and uncertainty.
2) Score each draft against the criteria and weights in input/rubric.yaml. Assign an integer score 0–5 for each criterion and compute a weighted total score = sum(score_i × weight_i). Use the decision thresholds in the rubric to label each draft as Minor revision, Moderate revision, or Major revision.
3) For each draft, identify the top two strengths and the top two concerns tied explicitly to the rubric criteria. Include 1–2 short evidence quotes (<=150 characters each) copied verbatim from the draft that substantiate your main concern(s).
4) Write a structured CSV to output/evaluations/reviews.csv with one row per draft and columns exactly in this order: file_name, student_name, title, hypothesis_score, methods_score, data_specificity_score, uncertainty_score, clarity_conciseness_score, weighted_total, decision, strengths (semicolon-separated), concerns (semicolon-separated), evidence_quotes (pipe-separated). Use input/recipients.csv to fill student_name by matching file_name.
5) Draft one personalized email per student that summarizes their result and gives concrete, actionable revision requests tied to missing or weak rubric elements. Save each email as a plain text file at output/emails/{student_lastname}_feedback.txt, where {student_lastname} is derived from the student_name field in input/recipients.csv (e.g., "H. Lee" -> "Lee"). Each email must include:
   - A subject line: "Preliminary feedback on your draft: {title}"
   - A brief opening addressed to the student by name
   - The weighted_total and decision
   - A concise list of the top two strengths and top two concerns
   - 1–2 short evidence quotes copied from their draft
   - 2–3 specific revision requests that clearly map to the rubric criteria
   - A professional but collegial tone

Deliverables to produce:
- output/evaluations/reviews.csv (structured scores and decisions)
- output/emails/Lee_feedback.txt
- output/emails/Sanchez_feedback.txt
- output/emails/Kim_feedback.txt

Please ensure that your scoring and decisions are traceable to the rubric, that the evidence quotes appear both in the CSV and in the respective email, and that names and email filenames align with input/recipients.csv. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
